description: Recto (secondary use): Letter to 'al-ḥaver al-meʿulle ḥakham ha-yeshiva" (possibly this is Mevorakh b. Saadya, although Goitein suggested that the letter was actually intended to be sent from Fustat to Palestine). In Judaeo-Arabic. Reporting that Abū Saʿd al-Ḥalabī brought a suftaja but Yeḥezqel denied owing anything to Muʿallā. The sender conveys the olives to the Nagid and figs to grandma. Sulṭān al-Dawla and 20 jars were not billed as the rumor had it. Mentions Abū l-Faraj b. Naḥum. Sitt Ihtimām will not sell the silver. Sitt Waṣl sends greetings.
description_he: מכתב ביהודית-ערבית ל'החבר המעולה חכם הישיבה'. מדווח על עסקאות שונות: זיתים לנגיד, תאנים לסבתא, ודיווחים על אנשים שונים.
